title: Trump's Bible reading is creating a stir. Why it's dividing
shortDescription: President Donald Trump read 2 Chronicles 7:11-22, Old Testament scripture, during a pre-recorded broadcast for America Reads the Bible on April 21.

title: Grindr hosting party ahead of White House dinner
shortDescription: Grindr, the LGBTQ dating app, is hosting its first-ever party ahead of the annual White House Correspondents Association Dinner.

title: Sculptor says golden Trump statue won’t be released unless he's paid
shortDescription: Ohio-based sculptor Alan Cottrill finished a 15-foot bronze statue of Donald Trump. But a fight over payment is getting international attention.

title: Key takeaways from Virginia redistricting vote
shortDescription: Virginians voted to craft an aggressive Democratic-leaning map for the state’s 11-member delegation to the House of Representatives.

title: Virginia votes 'yes' on high-stakes amendment: What happens next?
shortDescription: A new map stands to give Democrats robust support in Virginia.

title: Iran seizes 2 ships after Trump extends ceasefire: Iran war updates
shortDescription: Iran seized two ships, the first since the war began, as it discourages commerce through the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation for the U.S. blockade.

title: King Charles to visit US amid tensions with Trump
shortDescription: USA TODAY Washington Bureau Chief Susan Page dives into U.S. relations with the U.K. and what to expect as King Charles III visits Washington.

title: Trump reads scripture amid religious backlash
shortDescription: President Donald Trump read a passage from the Bible as part of a week of national scripture reading.

title: DOJ indicts Southern Poverty Law Center
shortDescription: The Justice Department announces an indictment accusing the Southern Poverty Law Center of fraud and funding extremist groups.

title: Trump to extend ceasefire with Iran
shortDescription: President Donald Trump announced he would extend the ceasefire with Iran until the country had submitted a peace proposal.

title: DOJ accuses Southern Poverty Law Center of funding KKK, other extremists
shortDescription: Southern Poverty Law Center leaders appeared to anticipate the indictment, promising "we will not be intimidated" in a statement before the indictment announcement.

title: Virginia voters approve major redistricting measure
shortDescription: Virginia voters have approved the redrawing of the state's congressional maps, NBC News and the Decision Desk HQ reported on Tuesday, April 21.

title: Grindr set to host inaugural White House Correspondents' Dinner party
shortDescription: Grindr is getting into the spirit of the White House Correspondents’ Association Dinner, hosting its first-ever party ahead of the annual event.

title: Tucker Carlson apologizes for supporting Trump
shortDescription: On an April 20 episode of “The Tucker Carlson Show,” Tucker Carlson said he is “implicated” in the Iran war for supporting President Donald Trump.

title: Trump praises Tim Cook after Apple CEO steps down
shortDescription: In a lengthy post, Trump described a "very nice" relationship with outgoing Apple CEO Tim Cook, who he said he had helped through his tenure.
